name: Billy
biography: Billy is Holmes's page — a live-in boy who answers the door, carries messages, and manages the domestic operations of 221B Baker Street in the later canon. He is described as a bright, discreet young man who takes quiet pride in his employer's methods and reputation. Unlike the Baker Street Irregulars, who are street children retained for specific outdoor tasks, Billy occupies a permanent position within the household and acts as an intermediary between Holmes and the outside world. He first appears in The Valley of Fear, announcing a visitor with cool efficiency, and recurs in The Adventure of the Mazarin Stone. Watson does not give him a surname. Holmes's domestic arrangements at 221B clearly evolved over the decades of the canon, and Billy represents the settled, post-Reichenbach period when Holmes was a figure of established fame.
